us.label: US News
us.subtitle: Top domestic developments from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Pentagon identifies two US soldiers killed in Iranian attack on Jordan
bullets: The Defense Department identified Tyler James Feehan of Hawaii and Isabella Gonzales of Texas.
Both were killed while defending against Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks in Jordan.
AP reported the deaths were the first US troop fatalities from direct Iranian fire since the war’s opening days.

articles.title: Five die in Ohio river rescue attempt
bullets: Authorities said five people died in the Scioto River near Powell, Ohio.
Several victims entered the water while trying to help a struggling swimmer.
A motorist called 911 after spotting a frightened child near the river.

articles.title: Judge pauses Paramount–Warner merger for at least two weeks
bullets: A federal judge ordered Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery to halt their merger temporarily.
A coalition of states says the deal could reduce competition in movies and cable TV.
The order gives the states more time to pursue their antitrust challenge.

articles.title: AP examines business group behind Trump-promoted Freedom Fuel
bullets: AP reported on the New Jersey businessmen connected to the Freedom Fuel gasoline brand.
The article reviewed prior business and legal issues tied to people behind the venture.
The fuel has been promoted in connection with Trump’s claims about lower gasoline prices.

articles.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on many Canadian goods
bullets: President Donald Trump announced 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods.
The White House cited disputes over autos, alcohol and dairy products.
The measure affects products such as wine, dairy, furniture, clothing, cement and hockey sticks.

global.label: Global
global.subtitle: Major worldwide stories and geopolitical developments

articles.title: Iran strikes Gulf sites as US completes 10th night of attacks
bullets: Iran said it struck US-linked military sites in Kuwait and Bahrain.
US Central Command said it completed a 10th consecutive night of strikes on Iran.
Two oil tankers caught fire in the Strait of Hormuz, according to IRGC claims reported by AnewZ.

articles.title: Houthis warn of Saudi shipping blockade in Red Sea
bullets: Yemen’s Houthis warned they may target ships going to and from Saudi ports.
AnewZ reported Saudi crude tankers rerouted after the warning.
The development raised concerns about Red Sea trade and energy supply routes.

articles.title: Meloni calls inquiry after Moroccan man dies in Italian police custody
bullets: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni called for a full investigation into the death.
The man died during an attempted arrest in Bologna.
Protests over the case led to clashes that injured police officers.

articles.title: Nicaragua’s Ortega says country will no longer hold elections
bullets: President Daniel Ortega declared Nicaragua would no longer hold elections.
He made the remarks during celebrations of the 1979 Sandinista revolution anniversary.
The statement further tightens the power-sharing arrangement with Rosario Murillo.

articles.title: Sri Lanka battles worst dengue outbreak in nearly a decade
bullets: Sri Lanka is facing its worst dengue outbreak in nearly 10 years.
Authorities deployed the military to help contain mosquito-borne infections.
Cases continued rising as public health teams expanded prevention measures.

india.label: India News
india.subtitle: Top India stories and policy developments

articles.title: Modi calls for ‘foolproof’ exam system after youth protests
bullets: Prime Minister Narendra Modi called for a stronger public examination system.
The comments followed clashes involving Cockroach movement supporters in Delhi.
Protesters are demanding action over exam paper leaks and irregularities.

articles.title: Delhi protests continue after clashes over exam leaks
bullets: Reuters reported more than 5,000 protesters gathered at Jantar Mantar on Tuesday.
Police said nearly 180 people were injured in Monday’s clashes, including officers and protesters.
The Cockroach Janta Party said it would continue protests but avoid another Parliament march.

articles.title: Police use tear gas to stop Cockroach movement march on Parliament
bullets: AP reported police used tear gas and batons to stop protesters in New Delhi.
Supporters demanded Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation.
The protest movement gained momentum after repeated exam paper leaks.

articles.title: India summons Russian diplomat over deaths of Indian seafarers
bullets: The Financial Express reported India summoned a Russian diplomat over a Ukraine ship attack.
Four Indian seafarers were killed near Ukraine’s Odesa port.
India condemned the attack on the merchant vessel MV Golden Leo.

articles.title: India adopts WTO pact banning support for illegal fishing
bullets: India became the 123rd member to join the WTO fisheries subsidies agreement.
The pact targets government support linked to illegal, unreported and unregulated fishing.
The move is aimed at sustainable fishing and protection for small-scale fishers.

stocks.label: US Stocks
stocks.subtitle: Major US market movers and trading themes

articles.title: AMD rises after expanded Microsoft AI partnership
bullets: AMD gained 1.6% after announcing an expanded partnership with Microsoft.
Microsoft will use AMD products for AI, including Helios starting in the second half of 2026.
The move came as investors watched AI infrastructure spending and megacap earnings.

articles.title: AMC jumps after stronger revenue report
bullets: AMC Entertainment rose 26.8% after reporting stronger-than-expected quarterly revenue.
The company cited demand tied to major film releases, including extended screenings of The Odyssey.
The move made AMC one of Monday’s notable single-stock gainers.

articles.title: Domino’s climbs on better-than-expected revenue
bullets: Domino’s Pizza rose 2.1% after spring revenue exceeded Wall Street expectations.
CEO Russell Weiner said carryout and delivery orders both grew.
The company noted broader restaurant industry pressure from consumer demand.

articles.title: AI chip stocks steady after prior week selloff
bullets: Nvidia added 0.2% after weighing heavily on the S&P 500 late last week.
Sandisk climbed 2.7% after tumbling 29% the previous week.
AI-linked stocks remained under scrutiny over valuation concerns.

articles.title: Warner Bros. Discovery falls after merger pause
bullets: Warner Bros. Discovery dropped 3.8% after a judge paused its Paramount deal.
The ruling helped erase an early S&P 500 gain.
States challenging the merger received more time to pursue their case.

articles.title: Paramount–Warner deal halted by federal judge
bullets: A federal judge stopped the merger for at least two weeks.
States argue the transaction could reduce competition in Hollywood and cable TV.
Paramount and Warner said the deal would strengthen competition against streaming rivals.

articles.title: AliExpress calls EU’s €550 million tech fine disproportionate
bullets: AliExpress objected to a record EU fine over illegal and counterfeit goods controls.
The company said the €550 million penalty was disproportionate.
EU regulators said the platform had not done enough after ordered fixes.

articles.title: Canada tariff move adds new pressure to North American trade
bullets: Trump imposed 50% tariffs on many Canadian imports.
The White House cited autos, alcohol and dairy disputes.
The tariffs cover a broad range of goods regardless of USMCA eligibility.

articles.title: Jersey Mike’s begins IPO roadshow
bullets: Jersey Mike’s began a roadshow to build investor interest in its planned IPO.
The restaurant chain plans to list on the New York Stock Exchange.
AP reported the expected offering range is $21 to $25 per share.

articles.title: UK nationalises British Steel to secure primary steelmaker
bullets: The UK government took full ownership of British Steel.
AnewZ reported the move is aimed at safeguarding the country’s only primary steelmaker.
China’s Foreign Ministry said it was monitoring the nationalisation after prior Chinese ownership by Jingye.

articles.title: Microsoft expands Azure AI infrastructure with AMD
bullets: Microsoft said it will bring AMD Helios and next-generation EPYC processors to Azure.
The partnership covers AI inference, data systems and high-performance computing workloads.
Microsoft said the systems will support new Azure HDv2, HXv2 and ND MI455X v7 offerings.

articles.title: More than 1,100 firms showcase AI at Shanghai WAIC
bullets: The World AI Conference in Shanghai featured more than 1,100 companies.
Organizers said over 3,000 products and technologies were on display.
China introduced new AI initiatives covering data, computing power, governance and ethics.

articles.title: Ex-Meta staff sue over alleged AI bias in layoffs
bullets: Twenty-six former Meta employees alleged AI-assisted systems unfairly targeted protected workers.
The lawsuit cites disability, pregnancy and family or medical leave protections.
Meta said workforce decisions were made by people, not AI.

articles.title: US says only small number of Nvidia H200 chips shipped to China
bullets: A senior US commerce official told lawmakers only a small number of H200 chips reached China.
The statement came amid scrutiny of advanced chip export controls.
The chips are used in artificial intelligence workloads.

articles.title: AI shopping tool Phia accused of affiliate commission abuse
bullets: AnewZ reported Phia was accused of affiliate commission ‘cookie stuffing.’
The allegation says the AI shopping assistant claimed commissions for purchases it did not generate.
The company said the issue has been resolved.
